accurate.why: All claims align with the bill text and accurate real-world context (e.g., reconciliation under S. Con. Res. 33).

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated or contradictory claims; figures and provisions match the bill.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the bill as a reconciliation measure funding border security and immigration enforcement.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Notes 287(g) agreements and statutory references like qualified cooperating jurisdiction compliance, consistent with the bill.

exceptions_conditions.why: Captures key conditions: processing-coordinator restriction, surveillance tower limit, tribal exclusion, and release limitations.

effective_dates.why: Correctly states funds remain available until September 30, 2029, and the October 31, 2028 restriction date.

major_provisions_covered.why: All appropriations sections and their essential operative effects are accurately summarized.

objective_tone.why: Summary is neutral and descriptive without editorializing.

coherent.why: Well-organized by title and section, internally consistent and clear.

concise.why: Efficient bulleted structure without padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: CBP, ICE, HSI, Secretary of Homeland Security, and 287(g) are all correctly identified.

correct_figures.why: All dollar amounts and dates ($9.55B, $7.45B, $108.5M, $3.45B, $2.5B, $13.02B, $31.075B, $350M, dates) match the bill.
